headline: GOLD HITS $4,748 — April Surge Signals Growing Anxiety Over U.S. Fiscal Stability
snippet: Gold touched $4,748/oz this week as investors flee fiscal uncertainty — with the U.S. running a $1.2T half-year deficit and national debt at $39T, analysts warn the Fed has limited room to tighten. Central bank demand is projected at 900 tonnes for full-year 2026.

headline: West Red Lake DRILLS 215 g/t Au Over 5.35m at Madsen — Austin 904 Complex Rivals Historic Red Lake Highs
snippet: West Red Lake Gold (TSXV: WRLG) returned 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m — including 388.90 g/t over 2.95m — at the Austin 904 Complex in Ontario's Red Lake district. Results exceed the historic Goldcorp High-Grade Zone benchmark; production targeted H1 2027.

headline: G Mining PAYS $2.2B for G2 Goldfields — Adjacent Guyana Assets Create 500k Oz Gold Hub
snippet: G Mining Ventures (TSX: GMIN) agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields (TSX: GTWO) in a 72%-premium all-share deal, consolidating two adjacent Guyana gold projects into a district with 500,000+ oz/yr potential and C$1B+ in identified synergies. Deal expected to close Q2 2026.

headline: Amex Exploration Posts C$1.13B NPV for Perron Gold Mine — 147,000 Oz/Yr at $910/Oz AISC in Quebec
snippet: Amex Exploration (TSXV: AMX) delivered a positive feasibility study for Phase 1 of the Perron Gold Mine in Abitibi, Quebec: 147,000 oz/yr over 5 years, C$1.13B post-tax NPV5 at 114.6% IRR, $910/oz AISC, and a low C$193.9M capex via toll milling. Revenue targeted by 2028.

headline: CHINA GRABS 790+ Tonnes of Silver in Early 2026 — Structural 67M Oz Deficit Drains Global Vaults
snippet: China's silver imports hit an eight-year high in early 2026 at over 790 tonnes, driven partly by solar panel manufacturing. The Silver Institute projects a 67M+ oz structural deficit for 2026 as COMEX registered inventories are down 70% since 2020 and LME vaults down 40%.

headline: NexGen STARTS BUILDING Rook I This Summer — Canada's Largest Uranium Deposit, 30M Lb/Yr Output
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX: NXE) received final federal approval from the CNSC in March and will break ground on Rook I in northern Saskatchewan this summer. At 30M lb U3O8/yr during peak production across an 11-year mine life, it would surpass Cameco's McArthur River as North America's top uranium mine.

headline: Core Lithium RESTARTS Finniss Open Pit in May — A$50M NRW Contract, First Concentrate Q4 2026
snippet: Core Lithium awarded a A$50M surface mining contract to NRW for the Grants pit at its Finniss Lithium Project in the Northern Territory; ore extraction begins May 2026 with processing starting July–September. The restart is backed by A$290M in new financing anchored by Glencore.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER Finds 10 New High-Grade Veins at Galena — 4,896 g/t Ag Highlights 64,000m Drill Push
snippet: Americas Gold & Silver (TSX: USA) discovered seven new silver-copper-antimony veins and three silver-lead veins at its Galena Complex in Idaho, with a highlight of 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m. The company is launching its largest-ever exploration program at approximately 64,000 metres.

headline: Ex-Rio CEO Tom Albanese MERGES Deep-Sea Miners in $1B Deal — U.S. Critical Minerals Platform Formed
snippet: American Ocean Minerals and Odyssey Marine Exploration announced a $1B all-stock merger to create a U.S.-controlled deep-sea platform targeting polymetallic nodules — rich in cobalt, nickel, copper and manganese. Ex-Rio Tinto CEO Tom Albanese will chair the combined Nasdaq-listed entity.

headline: USA RARE EARTH Invests in French Processor Carester — Transatlantic REE Supply Chain Takes Shape
snippet: USA Rare Earth has taken a strategic stake in Carester SAS, one of France's rare earth processing firms, as part of a push to build a mine-to-magnet supply chain across the Atlantic. The move comes with nine months left before U.S. defense procurement rules require the removal of Chinese REE content.

headline: China Reports 'STUNNING' Critical Minerals Finds — REE Race With U.S. Intensifies
snippet: Chinese geological surveys have revealed major new rare earth and critical mineral discoveries, according to state media, as Beijing's hi-tech competition with Washington accelerates. China already controls ~70% of global REE production and ~90% of refining capacity.

headline: GOLD-TO-SILVER Ratio Retreating From 105 Peak — Classic Bull Signal Flashing for Silver
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in early April before beginning to contract — a pattern that has historically marked the start of significant silver outperformance relative to gold. Analysts say this, combined with China demand and a structural supply deficit, sets up a strong silver trade.

headline: Copper's Next Supply Crisis Is STRUCTURAL, Not Cyclical — Analyst Makes the Case
snippet: A leading analyst argues the coming copper supply gap cannot be solved through a normal capex cycle: two decades of underinvestment in greenfield mines, worsening water constraints in Chile and DRC, and accelerating electrification demand point to a shortage that will outlast trade volatility.
